behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily wiring/state-handoff correctness across dispatcher + reviewer adapters. A unit test of one function can miss cross-module payload mapping bugs. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is only about what is sent ('sends ... correct ... instruction pack'), not whether model decisions are good. Rejected System because no explicit external contract/round-trip side effect is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process reviewer test doubles for extractor/risk/compliance; assert each invocation receives identical document bytes/content hash plus the role-appropriate instruction pack and metadata.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model extraction quality on legal text. Stubbed outputs would hide failures in identification/recall. Rejected Workflow because this behavior is a single reviewer capability, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real clause-extractor model on a labeled contract set and assert required key-term categories are correctly extracted within acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation plumbing/config propagation across modules; integration checks catch serialization/mapping/defaulting issues unit-isolated checks may miss. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets config passage ('passed correctly ... invocation'), not risk-judgment quality. Rejected System because no explicit real external response contract or round-trip side effect is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor call path with a capture double and verify policy id/version/rules and thresholds are transmitted exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly evaluates model judgment quality, especially nuanced phrasing detection; deterministic stubs cannot validate this. Rejected Workflow because this is one model step, not chained multi-agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk-assessor model on a curated set containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses and verify expected flags/labels against gold annotations.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module parameter handoff to the compliance-checker call boundary. Integration is the lowest level that validates assembled request context. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is only about passing inputs correctly, not model compliance reasoning quality. Rejected System because no explicit external contract or round-trip side-effect proof is requested.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke compliance-checker orchestration with test doubles and assert jurisdiction code, regulatory profile/version, and related context fields are present and correct in outgoing request payload.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: This is correctness of model-driven compliance determination, which depends on real AI output quality and can vary with real inputs. Stubs would create false confidence. Rejected Workflow because this behavior isolates one reviewer's decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real compliance-checker model on contracts with known regulatory outcomes across profiles and assert decision accuracy/coverage meets threshold.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: The risk is in schema normalization + merge-module interaction during conflict resolution and attribution retention. Integration validates inter-module handoff beyond single-function logic. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about merge preservation semantics once conflicting outputs exist, not whether model judgments are correct. Rejected System because no explicit external API/error contract or external round-trip side-effect requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic reviewer outputs from two+ modules into the merge pipeline (including conflicting labels on same clause id/span) and assert unified report keeps both assessments and reviewer attribution without overwrite.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires a full sequence of multiple agentic steps (three live reviewers) plus aggregation. Failures can emerge across chained model decisions and orchestration handoffs, so Workflow is required. Rejected Agentic because single-step model validation would not cover multi-step pipeline loss/coordination failures described in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline on a real contract using live extractor/risk/compliance calls; capture each reviewer output and verify final merged report contains every finding with attribution and no dropped items.
